# Title

Relationship Between Average Token Entropy (Bits/Token) and Character Error Rate (CER)

## Purpose of the Figure

This is the primary figure for the analysis. It shows the relationship between the two main variables of interest: as entropy increases, CER also tends to increase.

## Generated By

[entropy_vs_cer.py](../../scripts/entropy_vs_cer.py)

## Input Files Used

[results_k_10.csv](../../results/csv/results_k_10.csv)

## Output Filenames

| Format | File |
|---|---|
| PNG | [figure_01_entropy_vs_cer.png](../../results/figures/figure_01_entropy_vs_cer.png) |
| SVG | [figure_01_entropy_vs_cer.svg](../../results/figures/figure_01_entropy_vs_cer.svg) |

## X-axis

Average Token Entropy (Bits/Token)

## Y-axis

Character Error Rate (CER)

## Units

| Field | Units |
|---|---|
| Average Token Entropy | Bits/Token |
| Character Error Rate (CER) | % |

## Other Information

- Regression line: a line of best fit intended to summarize the relationship between entropy and CER.
- Color: the red regression line contrasts with the blue data points.
- Filtering rules: samples with a CER > 1% were filtered from the dataset.
- Normalization choices: whitespace was normalized, punctuation was removed entirely, and all characters were converted to lowercase for both ground-truth and OCR texts.
- Sample size: the plot illustrates the relationship between entropy and CER for 100 randomly selected page excerpts.

## Figure Importance

To demonstrate the relationship between entropy and CER.
